title: Build Structured Data Frame Subtypes
text: Provides developer-focused helper functions S3 classes ease creation structured subtypes data frames require certain columns types present, preserve special attributes common data frame manipulations.

title: Build Structured Data Frame Subtypes
text: can install development version schemar GitHub :

what: Define a structured data type — sch_schema
title: Define a structured data type — sch_schema
text: Defines structure single 'observation' structured data frame. column type restrictions may required optional. Schemas support nesting relationships.

title: Define a structured data type — sch_schema
code: sch_schema(..., .desc = NULL)  sch_others()  sch_nest(..., .keys = character(0), .desc = NULL, distinct = FALSE)  sch_numeric(   desc = NULL,   bounds = c(-Inf, Inf),   closed = c(TRUE, TRUE),   missing = TRUE,   required = TRUE,   distinct = FALSE )  sch_integer(   desc = NULL,   bounds = c(-Inf, Inf),   closed = c(TRUE, TRUE),   missing = TRUE,   required = TRUE,   distinct = FALSE )  sch_logical(desc = NULL, missing = TRUE, required = TRUE, distinct = FALSE)  sch_character(desc = NULL, missing = TRUE, required = TRUE, distinct = FALSE)  sch_factor(   desc = NULL,   levels,   strict = TRUE,   missing = TRUE,   required = TRUE,   distinct = FALSE )  sch_date(   desc = NULL,   bounds = c(as.Date(-Inf), as.Date(Inf)),   closed = c(FALSE, FALSE),   missing = TRUE,   required = TRUE,   distinct = FALSE )  sch_datetime(   desc = NULL,   bounds = c(as.POSIXct(-Inf), as.POSIXct(Inf)),   closed = c(FALSE, FALSE),   missing = TRUE,   required = TRUE,   distinct = FALSE )  sch_inherits(   desc = NULL,   class,   missing = TRUE,   required = TRUE,   distinct = FALSE )  sch_list_of(   desc = NULL,   class,   missing = TRUE,   required = TRUE,   distinct = FALSE )  sch_custom(   name,   desc = NULL,   check,   msg,   coerce,   ...,   missing = TRUE,   required = TRUE,   distinct = FALSE )

title: Define a structured data type — sch_schema
text: ... Column specifications, form col_name = col_type pairs, col_type call column type constructor listed , sch_numeric(). Every type must kind vector, .e., vctrs::obj_is_vector() must return TRUE. columns must named, except sch_others(), described , unnamed sch_nest(), describes set columns logically nested within outer columns stored flat actual data frame. named sch_nest() describes columns stored nested data frame. special function sch_others() indicates preferred location columns explicitly mentioned schema. sch_others() appears, columns allowed. Trailing commas permitted. .keys character vector selecting one column names ... serve key columns nested group. distinct TRUE, column must contain duplicate values (accounting nesting structure). FALSE (default), duplicates allowed. desc, .desc description column consumers schema. type contraints described separately need included description.  example "age", descriptoin might "Age patient years", "Non-negative integer representing age patient years". bounds, Length-two vector c(min, max) specifying allowed range values. closed Length-two logical vector specifying whether bounds closed (inclusive) open (exclusive). missing TRUE, column may contain missing values. Otherwise, missing values result error. required TRUE, column must present. FALSE, column optional. levels character vector factor levels. strict TRUE, factors specified levels accepted. FALSE, character vectors specified levels also accepted. class character vector class names. name name custom type. check two-argument function checks whether object satisfies type. first argument object check, second full type specification. msg one-argument function generates descriptive message type passed type object . end period. coerce two-argument function attempts coerce object type. first argument object coerce, second full type specification.

title: Define a structured data type — sch_schema
Define a structured data type — sch_schema
text: sch_others(): placeholder non-required columns schema. sch_nest(): set columns logically nested within outer schema. given name outer sch_schema(), columns stored nested data frame. unnamed, columns stored flat (adjacent outer columns). unique combinations rows defined .keys must repeat identically across groups defined outer columns. sch_numeric(): numeric vector optionally constrained within certain range. sch_integer(): integer vector optionally constrained within certain range. sch_logical(): logical vector. sch_character(): character vector. sch_factor(): factor specified levels. sch_date(): Date vector optionally constrained within certain range. sch_datetime(): POSIXct vector optionally constrained within certain range. sch_inherits(): list-column whose elements satisfy inherits(_, class). sch_list_of(): vector satisfying inherits(_, class). sch_custom(): custom type defined user-provided check, error, coercion functions. Additional Additional named values stored along type specification may passed via ... available check, error, coercion functions.

title: Define a structured data type — sch_schema
code: sch_schema(     .desc = "Student data",     age = sch_integer("Age in years", bounds = c(0, 130)),     birthday = sch_date("Date of birth", required = FALSE),     height = sch_numeric(         "Height in inches",         bounds = c(0, 108),         closed = c(FALSE, TRUE)     ),     teacher = sch_factor(levels = c("Jones", "Smith", "Hernandez")),     enrolled = sch_logical(missing = FALSE),     sch_others() ) #> Student data #> A schema with 4 required columns: #>      age  Age in years: An integer vector with values in [0, 130]. #> birthday  Date of birth: A date vector. [Optional] #>   height  Height in inches: A numeric vector with values in (0, 108]. #>  teacher  A factor; one of Jones, Smith, or Hernandez. #> enrolled  A logical vector. No NAs allowed. #>      ...  Other columns  sch_schema(     .desc = "MCMC draws",     draw = sch_integer(         "Draw number",         bounds = c(1, Inf),         closed = c(TRUE, FALSE),         distinct = TRUE     ),     sch_nest(         param = sch_factor("Parameter name", levels = c("mu", "sigma", "log_lik")),         value = sch_numeric("Parameter value"),         .keys = "param"     ) ) #> MCMC draws #> A schema with 2 required columns: #> draw  Draw number: An integer vector with values in [1, Inf). [Distinct] #>       (flat): #>       param  Parameter name: A factor; one of mu, sigma, or log_lik. #>       value  Parameter value: A numeric vector.  sch_custom(    name = "even",    check = function(x, type) is.integer(x) && all(x %% 2 == 0),    msg = function(type) "vector of even integers",    coerce = function(x, type) (as.integer(x) %/% 2) * 2 ) #> A vector of even integers

what: Validate a data frame against a schema — sch_validate
title: Validate a data frame against a schema — sch_validate
text: Checks data frame conforms schema, validating column presence, types, missing values, uniqueness constraints, nesting structure.

title: Validate a data frame against a schema — sch_validate
code: sch_validate(   schema,   data,   check = c("names", "types", "distinct", "nesting"),   call = rlang::caller_env() )

title: Validate a data frame against a schema — sch_validate
text: schema schema object created sch_schema(). data data frame validate. check character vector specifying checks perform. default runs checks. Possible values: "names": check missing required columns unexpected extra columns. "types": check column types missing-value (NA) constraints. "distinct": check uniqueness constraints columns marked distinct = TRUE. Omit large data frames check expensive. "nesting": validate nested schema columns (flat named nests), including key consistency. call environment call used error reporting, passed rlang::abort(). Useful wrapping sch_validate() inside another function error points right place.

title: Validate a data frame against a schema — sch_validate
text: data, invisibly, validation succeeds. Otherwise, error class sch_validation_error raised formatted summary issues found.

title: Validate a data frame against a schema — sch_validate
code: # Basic validation: valid data passes silently schema <- sch_schema(     id = sch_integer(distinct = TRUE),     name = sch_character(missing = FALSE),     age = sch_numeric(required = FALSE) ) df <- data.frame(id = 1:3, name = c("Alice", "Bob", "Carol"), age = c(25, NA, 30)) sch_validate(schema, df)  # Invalid, throw validation errors if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # missing required columns sch_validate(schema, data.frame(id = 1:2))  # Validation error: type constrains sch_validate(schema, data.frame(id = c(1L, 1L), name = c("Alice", NA))) } # }
